mission.title: linkerd: Dashboard's TrafficSplit section is producing an error
mission.description: Dashboard's TrafficSplit section is producing an error. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify linkerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your linkerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n linkerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
This is happening since `stable-2.12.0`, even after having installed the `linkerd-smi` extension which creates the TrafficSplit CRD.

The issue stems from #7334 where all TrafficSplit functionality was removed from the `web` service.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Dashboard's TrafficSplit section is producing an error
steps.description:
Hi !  To give some context here, this is part of ongoing work in Linkerd to move away from having SMI baked into Linkerd and instead be able to express much richer client policy (including traffic splits) using the gateway API.  You can read more about our motivation for that here
```yaml
linkerd v2.12.2
k8s version 1.23.5
linux (amd64)
Ubuntu 18.04.6 LTS
```

steps.title: Confirm Dashboard's TrafficSplit section is producing an… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n linkerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Hi !  To give some context here, this is part of ongoing work in Linkerd to move away from having SMI baked into Linkerd and instead be able to express much richer client policy (including traffic splits) using the gateway API.
codeSnippets:
linkerd v2.12.2
k8s version 1.23.5
linux (amd64)
Ubuntu 18.04.6 LTS

# linkerd viz stat ts/my-split
Error: error creating metrics request while making stats request: cannot find Kubernetes canonical name from friendly name [ts]
Usage:
  linkerd viz stat [flags] (RESOURCES)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
